The '''Mission to Zhar''' was a mission on the part of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] to retrieve [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] [[Tydirium/Legends|''Tydirium'']] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]].

==Prelude==
After they had captured the [[Death Star II plans]] from the freighter ''[[Suprosa]]'', the Alliance tasked a team of [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] spies to infiltrate the Death Star operation and discover what information they could. After a daring rescue operation to retrieve the Bothans on their return, the Alliance discovered enough information to begin their plans. Key to the strategy was obtaining the necessary codes to enter the [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] system, fortunately for them, the Alliance had learned of an Imperial shuttle that carried the codes they needed. They set in motion an audacious plan to steal the shuttle from a heavily guarded Imperial outpost

==The mission==
The starship tasked with this dangerous operation was the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] Cruiser [[Liberty/Legends|''Liberty'']], who had among her ranks a pilot whose talents were perfect for the operation. This pilot was [[Ace Azzameen/Legends|Ace Azzameen]], whose experience flying freighters, particularly in dangerous situations, was unparalleled among ''Liberty''{{'s}} pilots.

Leaving ''Liberty'' in his customized [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|YT-1300]], the ''[[Sabra]]'', Ace set out for the [[Zhar system]], with a team of commandos onboard. Before he could enter the system itself, Ace had to pass through the [[Imperial Office of Customs/Legends|Imperial Customs]] [[Golan III Space Defense NovaGun|Golan III]] station at the edge of the system. After transmitting a false ship's manifest that the Alliance had provided, there followed an agonizing wait as the manifest was verified, all the while Ace had to use all of his piloting ability to avoid getting to close to the patrolling [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]] while "remaining casual."

Eventually, the ''[[Sabra]]'' was given clearance to proceed. To Ace's surprise, just as he was preparing to make a brief hyperjump in-system, the [[Victory I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory I''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Inexorable (Victory-class)|''Inexorable'']] arrived. Ace made the jump quickly, before ''Inexorable'' could identify him, and his true cargo.
[[File:327inter.jpg|thumb|left|260px|The interior of the Outpost. ''Tydirium'' can be seen on the right of the tunnel.]]
When Ace arrived in-system, he found the Imperial outpost, [[Outpost 327]], to be surrounded by a huge amount of TIE Fighters, freighters and cargo containers. The [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Judicator/Legends|''Judicator'']] was also present in the area. Again, Ace found himself having to avoid the Imperial fighters as he made his way to Outpost 327's hangar, where the ''Tydirium'' was docked. Ace succeeded, despite a near fatal mistake by the facility's commander, who failed to recognize the ''[[Sabra]]'' on his roster for several minutes.

The mission was further complicated by events after ''Sabra'' docked. The commando team was slow in getting the ''Tydirium'' ready for launch, causing ''Sabra'' to be docked far longer than ''[[Fiery Dawn]]'', the next freighter of schedule was happy with, this caused Ace's co-pilot, [[MK-09/Legends|Emkay]], considerable consternation as he made excuses in an attempt to keep the command center sending a team down. Just as the commandos were finished, the outpost's commander sent out and order to seize and detain the ''Sabra''. Their time up, Ace blasted his way out of the hangar and called in [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]].

The ''Judicator'' and TIE starfighters swarmed the Rebels, in an attempt to stop them. Meanwhile, [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE/sa bombers]] were scrambled to intercept the ''Tydirium''. However, Ace was able to stop them and allow the ''Tydirium'' to escape into hyperspace, a few seconds later, Ace and Rogue Squadron followed.

==Aftermath==
{{Quote|We have stolen a small Imperial shuttle. Disguised as a cargo ship, and using a secret Imperial code, a strike team will land on the moon and deactivate the shield generator.|[[General/Legends|General]] [[Crix Madine/Legends|Crix Madine]]|audio=Madine-Imperialshuttlestolen.flac|<ref name="RotJ">{{Film|VI}}</ref>}}
However, the Empire somehow retrieved the shuttle sometime later, possibly intercepting the ship before it reached the Rebel fleet. As a result, she was re-captured by [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] during the [[Mission to Prefsbelt IV]].

Alliance General [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] was able to use the ''Tydirium'' to infiltrate the [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] system along with his commandos, prompting the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]].

==Behind the scenes==
The games ''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron II: Rogue Leader]]'' and ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance]]'', the only two sources to detail the shuttle's capture, give conflicting information. [[Leland Chee]] has confirmed that both happened, and ''Mission to Zhar'' came first.<ref name="Chee" />

In the book ''[[Star Wars: Complete Cross-Sections]]'', it is stated that the codes were retrieved by Ace at Zhar, but the shuttle itself was stolen by Wedge Antilles during the mission on Prefsbelt IV.
